evidence.q1: Sections numbered 1 through 15 sequentially; no internal cross‑references appear to be broken.
evidence.q2: 'Personal Data' defined in Section 2 as 'raw individual records...' and used consistently capitalized, e.g., Section 3 'Vendor will process Personal Data only...'.
evidence.q3: Time periods are specific: 'within 24 hours', '2 business days', '10 business days', '30 days', '365 days', no vague terms.
evidence.q4: No archaic or out‑of‑register diction observed; language is modern throughout, e.g., 'Vendor will process Personal Data only on documented instructions'.
evidence.q5: All sentences are grammatical and non‑contradictory, e.g., 'Vendor will notify Customer without undue delay and in any event within 24 hours after discovering a Personal Data Breach.'
evidence.q6: Consistent register across sections: use of 'Vendor will' for obligations, plain contractual language in every section.
